mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k in a month signals strong labor market demand, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production above 110 index points indicates robust manufacturing activity, increasing corporate margins and equity valuations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain negative despite INDPRO exceeding 110 index points for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 800k units signals strong consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, boosting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where HOUST exceeds 800k units.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 2%) creates stagflation risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to eroding real returns and policy uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 220 and FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 375 billion) during late-cycle expansions signals overheating demand, prompting monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive when RSAFS exceeds 375 billion for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth above 2% indicates strong macroeconomic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through sustained corporate earnings and investor confidence.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative over 60 days despite GDPC1 growth exceeding 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment below 5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.
